title: EUTxO Fundamentals
description: The transaction model that makes Cardano smart contracts work.

stops.title: The Extended UTxO Model
stops.narrative: Cardano extends Bitcoin's UTxO model with three key additions: [datums](node:datum) for state, [redeemers](node:redeemer) for user actions, and [script context](node:script-context) for transaction introspection. This makes UTxOs programmable while keeping transactions deterministic — you know the outcome before submitting.

stops.title: Datums: Contract State
stops.narrative: A [datum](node:datum) is data attached to a UTxO locked by a script. It represents the contract's state. With [inline datums](node:inline-datums) (CIP-32), the full datum lives on-chain — anyone can read the contract state by querying the UTxO.

stops.title: On-Chain Execution
stops.narrative: [Plutus Core](node:plutus-core) is the low-level lambda calculus that runs on-chain. The [CEK machine](node:cek-machine) evaluates it, tracking [ExUnits](node:exunits) (CPU + memory) against the transaction budget. Exceeding the budget = transaction fails.

stops.title: What Scripts Cost
stops.narrative: [Cost models](node:cost-models) assign costs to each Plutus built-in function. These are [protocol parameters](node:param-group-technical) that governance can update via [parameter change actions](node:action-param-change). ExUnits are converted to ada fees using price parameters.
